tweets.text:
I love Jeremy's idea of the billion dollar PDF.

Every so often someone crystallizes an idea at just the right moment.

It sets the narrative for that era and billions of dollars organize around it.

- The Bitcoin white paper
- Situational Awareness
- Attention Is All You Need
- Brian Arthur's Increasing Returns
- Software is Eating the World

tweets.text:
Jeremy's ideas are always original

My favorite from this one is "peak guy" and why the poaster is our new priestly class

"I think the billionaire class has become subservient to the poasting class.

It doesn't make sense that we spend so much time caring about what billionaires think about theology or health or topics unrelated to accruing a billion dollars.

The reason we do is because these are the people that have ascended to the highest realm of piety in our value system.

We've looked to them to provide us these answers and it has not been satisfying.

Peak guy is I think we've had enough of the billionaire.

The logical question is what is the next class, our next set of priests? I think it's the poaster.

You can always look which class is subservient to the next to see who's next.

I was at this thing with a bunch of billionaire investors and they were all fighting over who could sit next to @tylercowen because he's the most interesting person there."

tweets.text:
I can't stress enough how much liquidity fragmentation is hurting crypto in the long run.

I'm always on the hunt for new projects that are trying to make markets more active, easier to use, and less fragmented.

So let me explain why @WaterX_app is the latest project I put on my watchlist:

They launched on @SuiNetwork earlier this month with ~30 perp markets and are backed by Sui's Moonshots Program.

The interesting part: they're building an AI-native trading platform that combines perps and their newly launched Beta prediction market in one place.

And I like that WaterX Predict Beta has a "swipe to predict" feature, which simplifies the process with a mechanic most people already understand  (like swiping left or right on a dating app).

On top of that, you can share your position as a gift link, drop it to a friend, claim the prediction, and pass it on.

That said, with the 2026 World Cup live, that mechanic makes sense because it's built for social distribution, and if you're watching the matches anyway, it's probably a good time to pick a market and try the beta.

They're also running a month-long Predict Festival alongside the tournament: make a prediction any day, and you earn a chest; the more days you stay active, the higher the tier climbs, all opening at the end of the month. Trades also earn a USD reward of up to 10% of your volume. Low-commitment way to test it while the matches are on.

Check it out here: https://t.co/0LvRFnen8j
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
WaterX Predict Festival

A month of predictions. A month of chests piling up. Powered by @SuiNetwork.

🔹 Jul 7 – Aug 7
🔹 The more days you predict, the more and better chests you get
🔹 Earn USD rewards of up to 10% of your volume on each trade

Start now: https://t.co/a6QO68An7a
tweets.full_text:
I can't stress enough how much liquidity fragmentation is hurting crypto in the long run.

I'm always on the hunt for new projects that are trying to make markets more active, easier to use, and less fragmented.

So let me explain why @WaterX_app is the latest project I put on my watchlist:

They launched on @SuiNetwork earlier this month with ~30 perp markets and are backed by Sui's Moonshots Program.

The interesting part: they're building an AI-native trading platform that combines perps and their newly launched Beta prediction market in one place.

And I like that WaterX Predict Beta has a "swipe to predict" feature, which simplifies the process with a mechanic most people already understand  (like swiping left or right on a dating app).

On top of that, you can share your position as a gift link, drop it to a friend, claim the prediction, and pass it on.

That said, with the 2026 World Cup live, that mechanic makes sense because it's built for social distribution, and if you're watching the matches anyway, it's probably a good time to pick a market and try the beta.

They're also running a month-long Predict Festival alongside the tournament: make a prediction any day, and you earn a chest; the more days you stay active, the higher the tier climbs, all opening at the end of the month. Trades also earn a USD reward of up to 10% of your volume. Low-commitment way to test it while the matches are on.

Check it out here: https://t.co/0LvRFnen8j

DCs are normally in rural areas or industrial parks and nowhere near residential housing. if they end up near neighborhoods that's a zoning mistake, not a data center problem. they don't actually make that much noise either; the videos you see obviously don't prove anything, audio levels can be trivially manipulated. 

the water use thing is a complete non-factor not worth wasting any time on; spend three seconds looking it up.

5. crypto industry less credible overall

tweets.text:
Gavin Newsom’s Presidential Chances are Plummeting. Nate Silver Explains Why:

“Newsom has fallen in Democratic primary polls from around 25% to 15%. He's fallen on @Polymarket from like 33% to 22%.”

Newsom’s biggest problems:

-- His early lead was based on name brand, which has eroded

-- Disastrous governance record in California

-- Aligning with Biden and continuity with the unpopular "Resistance Libs"

-- Dem voters now favor younger, more compelling candidates (AOC, Ossoff)

@NateSilver538:

“I think he was benefiting from some degree of name recognition, whereas now Democrats see a candidate like Jon Ossoff in Georgia, another good-looking young guy, but who actually has credentials which Newsom will never have, having won an election in a purple state.

I think it's good that Democrats are showing a bias toward younger candidates, right?

That's a lot of what happened with Zohran, too.

I wouldn't discount, by the way, someone like an AOC (as the 2028 nominee).

I think ultimately Newsom has a hard argument to make. He's arguing for continuity with Kamala Harris and Joe Biden. He's embracing Joe Biden when that strategy has failed electorally.

I would argue in California governance-wise as well. I think Newsom is actually in a pretty defensive position.”

---------------------------------------

Thanks to our partners for making this possible!

tweets.text:
My partner Steve Jurvetson spent his nights and weekends launching rockets. Totally obsessed.

So I told him, "Find us the best rocket company."

Three weeks later he came back.

"The best rocket company is Elon's."

My first reaction: "Isn't he supposed to be running Tesla?" We already had a big investment in Tesla, but we heard him out.

In that meeting, you could feel it. His mission in life was to make humanity multi-planetary. Tesla was a means to an end.

So we became the first money in after Elon's own $100 million and an investment from Founders Fund.

Just before we invested, the first few rockets had exploded on the launch pad..  but then a Falcon 1 lifted off and worked. It launched one small satellite for Malaysia, and then it was retired.

Falcon 9 was the true workhorse, which good customers came for later over time.

Eventually there were contracts everywhere, and Elon Musk began his journey to make us multiplanetary.

A mission like "we're going to Mars" attracts the best engineers in the world. People take a new job to solve a problem they'll tell their grandkids about.

When Kennedy said we choose to go to the moon, the technologies that came out of that effort changed everything on Earth.

Same thing is happening now. Starlink exists because Elon Musk is building to get us to Mars.

If the mission is grand enough, innovations that happen along the way will enhance and may even redefine the business.

Thanks to Bonnie Blockchain for having me on!

So I told him, "Find us the best rocket company."

My first reaction: "Isn't he supposed to be running Tesla?" We already had a big investment in Tesla, but we heard him out.

tweets.text: After seeing Steve Jurvetson's resume, I was almost sold, but when I met him, he knocked me over with his intelligence, his bravery and his sense of fun. He had such enthusiasm for ultimate frisbee and mountain biking, I knew he wasn't just an academic. He had a zest for life, adventure and people. One thing people didn't notice on his resume was that he was not only an RJ Miller scholar at GSB, but he won the "nice guy" award as the most popular biz school student among his peers. And the proof is in the pudding. He and Elon built a relationship that got us a couple trinicorns in Tesla and SpaceX.
tweets.full_text: After seeing Steve Jurvetson's resume, I was almost sold, but when I met him, he knocked me over with his intelligence, his bravery and his sense of fun. He had such enthusiasm for ultimate frisbee and mountain biking, I knew he wasn't just an academic. He had a zest for life, adventure and people. One thing people didn't notice on his resume was that he was not only an RJ Miller scholar at GSB, but he won the "nice guy" award as the most popular biz school student among his peers. And the proof is in the pudding. He and Elon built a relationship that got us a couple trinicorns in Tesla and SpaceX.

tweets.text: Goede argumenten zijn altijd welkom Michiel, maar die zie ik niet bij je. Als je dan ook nog onzin verkoopt over waarom je denkt dat ik in SGP woon, dan kun je beter meteen ophouden, want dan ben je je geloofwaardigheid al kwijt. Maar goed, je gelooft blijkbaar dat geld beter door de overheid uitgegeven kan worden dan door mensen die er eigenlijk recht op hebben, en dat je daarmee diefstal van mensen die overleden zijn goedkeurt. Dat zijn wat mij betreft communistische trekjes, die ik zojuist 'toevallig' ook op het NOS journaal voorbij zag komen (nog een bewijs van het brainwashen dat hier gebeurt, en waar jij volgens mij een slachtoffer van bent). Heel eng hoe dat hier in NL gaat tegenwoordig en vooral dat veel mensen het niet eens (willen) zien.

tweets.text:
Recap of the past 2 weeks in crypto:

- $BTC managed to hold the line at $58k after several breakdown attempts, and despite intense MSTR+STRC FUD leading to both asset dumping hard. Peak FUD seems behind us, STRC bounced from $72 to $90, and BTC is around $64k

- $LIT has been the strongest coin lately, it properly broke out above $2 after the news that it was the official perp DEX of the Robinhood Chain.

- Moderate SOL season with a +25% move on SOLBTC, as well as the ANSEM memecoin pumping from 0 to $400M mcap. FARTCOIN bounced too. $USELESS remains the best looking memecoin

- $SYN squeezed 25X from bottom to top after its repositioning as an options protocol

- $VVV is bleeding since it was announced that Venice raised $70M but in Venice equity

- Memory stocks are getting totally hammered (-30%) and the semis sector is selling off

- $MON is bouncing (... for no reason?)

When is the SOL coming back to San Francisco?! 

Well, this Thursday, and it’s an amazing model. $5 input, $30 output; it’s excellent for long-running tasks and doesn’t need too much guidance to find the right way to get the job done!! https://t.co/2W8O6vHWMi
